questions.question: The ancient Egyptians used $\frac{1}{\sqrt{2}}$ to represent a fraction. Rationalize the denominator.
The Mayans used $\frac{3}{\sqrt{5}}$ in their calendar calculations. Rationalize the denominator.
The ancient Greeks rationalized $\frac{2}{\sqrt{3}}$ as $\frac{2\sqrt{3}}{3}$. What is the value of $\frac{2\sqrt{3}}{3}$ if $\sqrt{3}=1.73$?
The Babylonians used $\frac{5}{\sqrt{6}}$ in their astronomical observations. Rationalize the denominator.
The ancient Chinese used $\frac{4}{\sqrt{8}}$ in their mathematical texts. Rationalize the denominator.
The ancient Indians used $\frac{6}{\sqrt{10}}$ in their mathematical discoveries. Rationalize the denominator.
The ancient Sumerians used $\frac{8}{\sqrt{12}}$ in their economic transactions. Rationalize the denominator.
The ancient Phoenicians used $\frac{9}{\sqrt{15}}$ in their maritime trade. Rationalize the denominator.
The ancient Egyptians built the Great Pyramid using $\frac{10}{\sqrt{20}}$ as a ratio of base to height. Rationalize the denominator and simplify.
The ancient Aztecs used $\frac{12}{\sqrt{24}}$ in their architectural designs. Rationalize the denominator and simplify.

teacher_rules: Tip 1: Ensure students understand the historical context of rationalizing denominators.
Tip 2: Encourage students to simplify their answers.
Tip 3: Remind students to check their work for accuracy.
